title: L-Network Impedance Matching at 1 MHz
description: Design and verify a passive L-network for impedance matching between a 50-ohm source and a 200-ohm load at 1 MHz. The generated circuit must be SPICE-simulatable, measurable through .meas directives, and evaluated under nominal and tolerance-aware conditions.
objective: Obtain a passive L-section matching network such that the input impedance seen by the source is approximately 50 ohms at 1 MHz and the reflection coefficient magnitude remains sufficiently small.

llm_task.expected_output: A valid ngspice-compatible netlist implementing a passive L-network with explicit source and load, AC analysis, and .meas directives for input impedance and reflection coefficient metrics.
llm_task.topology_hint: Use a two-reactive-element L-section composed of one inductor and one capacitor between the source and the resistive load.

measurements.name: zin_real
measurements.description: Real part of the input impedance at 1 MHz.

measurements.name: zin_imag
measurements.description: Imaginary part of the input impedance at 1 MHz.

measurements.name: zin_mag
measurements.description: Magnitude of the input impedance at 1 MHz.

measurements.name: gamma_mag
measurements.description: Magnitude of the reflection coefficient at 1 MHz.

all_of.rationale: The real part of the input impedance should remain close to the desired 50-ohm match.
The real part of the input impedance should remain close to the desired 50-ohm match.
The input impedance should be approximately real at the target frequency.
The input impedance should be approximately real at the target frequency.
The reflection coefficient magnitude must remain small enough to indicate acceptable matching.

robustness.description: Tolerance-aware robustness assessment under reactive component variations.
robustness.robust_acceptance_rule: All nominal acceptance criteria must remain satisfied for every sweep combination.

decision_protocol.run_definition: The generated netlist parses and executes successfully in ngspice, and all required nominal measurements are extractable.
decision_protocol.pass_definition: The design satisfies all nominal acceptance criteria at 1 MHz.
decision_protocol.robust_pass_definition: The design satisfies all nominal acceptance criteria under all robustness sweep conditions.

checks: Source resistance must be explicitly present and equal to 50 ohms.
Load resistance must be explicitly present and equal to 200 ohms.
The circuit must implement a passive two-reactive-element L-network.
The AC grid must include the target frequency of 1 MHz.
Input impedance must be computed with a consistent current sign convention.
The reflection coefficient must be defined with respect to the 50-ohm reference impedance.

primary_selection_metric.name: gamma_mag
primary_selection_metric.description: Among passing candidates, prefer the design with the smallest reflection coefficient magnitude at 1 MHz.

secondary_selection_metrics.name: zin_imag_abs
secondary_selection_metrics.description: Prefer candidates whose input impedance is closer to purely real at 1 MHz.

expected_failure_modes: Incorrect computation of input impedance due to current sign convention.
Reflection coefficient defined with the wrong reference impedance.
Target frequency not included on the AC simulation grid.
Use of an invalid or non-passive topology.
Nominal pass achieved but robustness failure under inductor or capacitor tolerance.

metadata.paper_alignment: Reproducible SPICE-based verification of LLM-generated passive matching networks

impedance.description: If zin_real is missing but zin_mag exists, and |zin_imag| <= threshold (or missing), set zin_real = zin_mag.

reflection.description: If gamma_mag is missing, compute it from zin = zin_real + j*zin_imag and Z0.
